Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low serum protein, and low globulin), mild liver enzyme elevation (ALT), and abnormal urine findings (protein, glucose, ketones, low specific gravity, brown color, ammoniacal odor). These findings suggest possible chronic kidney disease with nephrotic-range proteinuria and possible metabolic derangements. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.
